title: Mass spectrometry system and method with controlled ion transfer
abstract: An ion transfer device of a mass spectrometry system that includes a first ion guide such that the first ion guide traps the ions in a first potential well formed by a first potential gradient and a first potential barrier within the first ion guide, and a second ion guide that receives the ions from the first ion guide if the first potential barrier is reduced such that during a time period when the ions travel toward one end of the second ion guide, a second potential barrier is gradually formed by increasing a third voltage applied to a third electrode at the one end of the second ion guide, the second potential barrier preventing the ions from exiting the second ion guide or from colliding with the third electrode.
claims: 1. A mass spectrometry system comprising: an ion source that produces ions; a first ion guide that guides or traps the ions within the first ion guide by applying one or more first voltages to a plurality of first electrodes of the first ion guide, wherein the first ion guide traps the ions in a first potential well formed by a first potential gradient and a first potential barrier within the first ion guide, and wherein the ions move out of the first ion guide if the first potential barrier is reduced; a second ion guide that receives the ions from the first ion guide if the first potential barrier is reduced, and guides or traps the ions within the second ion guide by applying one or more second voltages to a plurality of second electrodes of the second ion guide, wherein, during a time period when the ions travel toward one end of the second ion guide, a second potential barrier is gradually formed by increasing a third voltage applied to a third electrode at the one end of the second ion guide, wherein the third voltage of the third electrode gradually increases from one voltage value to another voltage value in a predetermined time period, the time period being between 0.01 to 1000 milliseconds, wherein the second potential barrier is formed before the ions reach the third electrode to prevent the ions from exiting the second ion guide or from colliding with the third electrode, and wherein the second ion guide traps the ions, and the ions form an ion packet within the second ion guide; and a mass analyzer that separates the ions of the ion packet based on mass-to-charge ratio of the ions.
